commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: Already chained to evil, they wanted if possible to devour the good, so that just as a sepulchre is open to receive corpses, so their throat is open to devour the good…. The words of men are like tiny mice. They speak in order to deceive, and just as poison flows from the lips of a serpent, so trickery and deceit flow from their lips. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Their feet are swift to shed blood, destruction and misery are in their ways, who have not known the way of peace; neither is the fear of God before their eyes."

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: "Anger has been prohibited, our spirits retained, the petulance of the hand checked, the poison of the tongue

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
